source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Ruth and Sarah decide to have a race. Ruth runs around the perimeter of the pool shown in the diagram while Sarah swims lengths of the pool.
Ruth runs three times as fast as Sarah swims. Sarah swims six lengths of the pool in the same time Ruth runs around the pool five times. How wide is the pool?
<image1>
extraction_notes: choice labels inferred from options array order to map answer B to option text

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'the pool shown in the diagram'
image_quality_summaries readability_score=0.8187 suggests the diagram is usable
multi_step: question_text combines at least two relations: Ruth runs three times as fast as Sarah swims, and Sarah swims six lengths in the same time Ruth runs around the pool five times
solving requires linking pool perimeter to pool lengths and then isolating the pool width
the problem is quantitative and not answerable by direct lookup from the text alone
multiple-choice options are provided, which supports a structured reasoning target
verifiability: answer_type=short_text with a specific numeric result ('$40 \mathrm{~m}$')
choices include an exact matching option (B)
the result can be checked by algebra from the stated relations plus the diagram information

decision_reasons: question, answer, and primary diagram are all present, so the core semantics are available
initial scores are strong for image dependency, multi-step reasoning, and verifiability
the flagged diagram-specific dimension is recoverable because the referenced image exists and appears usable
no registry issues were reported and the sample is suitable for the candidate pool
